title: Both of Us — The TH at the End
description: Final-position TH: BOTH, WITH, FIFTH, MONTH, EARTH, SOUTH. Bia DJs on the rooftop and every song brings a new TH to notice.
summary_pt_br: O TH no FIM das palavras (o mais esquecido): BOTH, WITH, FIFTH, MONTH, EARTH, SOUTH. Treinado com With or Without You (U2), Both Sides Now (Joni Mitchell) e What a Wonderful World (Louis Armstrong).

scenes.subtitle_en: TH at the end — don't drop it

scenes.text_en:
Bia set up speakers on the rooftop deck.
'Today's song lesson? A TH tech-check.
BOTH of us will sing. WITH our best,
We'll hit the FIFTH, the MONTH, the rest.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Bia instalou caixas no terraço da escola.
'Aula musical de hoje? TH na viola.
BOTH de nós vamos cantar. WITH o nosso melhor,
Acertando FIFTH, MONTH, e todo o teor.'

scenes.title: Final-TH is the hardest — don't drop it
scenes.body_en: Brazilians often drop or soften the TH at the end of words because Portuguese rarely ends words in that position. Don't. 'Both' has THREE sounds: /b/ + /oʊ/ + /θ/. The /θ/ at the end is small but essential. Give it a clear blow of air.
scenes.body_pt: Brasileiros tendem a engolir o TH no fim das palavras porque o português quase não tem som assim nessa posição. Não engole. 'Both' tem TRÊS sons: /b/ + /oʊ/ + /θ/. O /θ/ final é pequeno, mas crítico. Solte o arzinho com clareza.

scenes.text_en:
U2 sings: 'I can't live WITH or WITHOUT you.'
Joni Mitchell sang: 'I've looked at clouds from BOTH sides now.'
And Louis Armstrong: 'I see trees of green, red roses too,
I see them bloom for me and for you… what a wonderful WORLD.'
scenes.text_pt:
U2 canta: 'I can't live WITH or WITHOUT you' (Não consigo viver COM nem SEM você).
Joni Mitchell cantou: 'I've looked at clouds from BOTH sides now' (Já olhei para as nuvens por AMBOS os lados).
E Louis Armstrong: 'I see trees of green… what a wonderful WORLD' — até 'world' tem um eco de TH (na verdade é só 'd', mas 'wonder' tem /ð/).

exercise.question: Complete the U2 line: 'I can't live ___ or without you.'
exercise.explanation: WITH /wɪð/ — voiced TH at the end. Even Bono drops it sometimes in fast singing, but say it clearly in speech.
exercise.hint_pt_br: WITH /wɪð/ — /ð/ vibrado no final. Mesmo o Bono abafa em músicas rápidas, mas na fala é pra sair.

exercise.question: 'I want ___ options.' (= os dois)
exercise.explanation: BOTH /boʊθ/ ends with /θ/. 'Bot' (robô) and 'boss' (chefe) mean something else.
exercise.hint_pt_br: BOTH termina com /θ/. 'Bot' é robô; 'boss' é chefe. Conferir o final é crítico.

turns.en: This song is from the fifth month of the year.
turns.pt: Essa música é do quinto mês do ano.
Maio? É o mês número CINCO.

turns.en: Right — the FIFTH month. Not the fift. The FIFTH.
turns.pt: Exato — o QUINTO mês. Não 'fift'. É FIFTH.

turns.en: FIFTH. I got it. Three sounds at the end: /f-θ/.
turns.pt: FIFTH. Peguei. Três sons no final: /f-θ/.

exercise.explanation: Five final-TH words. Chain them: 'Both of us went south from Earth one month — with a dog.'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco palavrinhas com TH no final. Frase de treino: 'Both of us went south from Earth one month — with a dog.'

exercise.question: Which of these words ends with the VOICED /ð/?
exercise.explanation: WITH ends with /ð/ (voiced — hum). The others (both, month, south) end with /θ/ (voiceless — just air). This is the one word that breaks the pattern.
exercise.hint_pt_br: WITH é diferente: termina com /ð/ vibrado. Os outros (both, month, south) terminam em /θ/ mudo.

scenes.text_en:
Mr. Kumar clapped his hands. 'BOTH sounds, WITH and without voice.
The MONTH we spent on TH was worth it, no choice.
You can feel it on EARTH. You can feel it in the SOUTH.
You can feel it in every English-speaking mouth.'
scenes.text_pt:
Mr. Kumar bateu palmas. 'AMBOS os sons, COM e sem voz.
O MÊS que gastamos com TH valeu, sem mais algoz.
Vocês sentem na TERRA. Sentem no SUL.
Sentem em toda boca que fala inglês — sem mais álcool.'

scenes.title: Planning a trip with a friend

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hey. We should plan our trip.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes. Both of us are free in July.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Nice. Do you prefer beach or mountains?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Me too. Neither is too expensive.
turns.pt: Eu também. Nenhum é caro demais.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: How about we do both places?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Great idea. Beach first or mountains first?
turns.pt: Ótima ideia. Praia ou montanha primeiro?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Let's do beach first, then mountains.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Perfect. I am booking both hotels tonight.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Awesome. This is going to be amazing.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Both of Us — The TH at the End". Final-position TH: BOTH, WITH, FIFTH, MONTH, EARTH, SOUTH. Bia DJs on the rooftop and every song brings a new TH to notice. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Both of Us — The TH at the End". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
Both of Us — The TH at the End
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Both of Us — The TH at the End'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Both of Us — The TH at the End".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
